plan: Conduct literature review on LLM judges, agent evaluation, and error propagation
Design minimal POC scope (dataset, perturbations, metrics)
Define success criteria and experimental protocol
completed_steps: Conduct literature review on LLM judges, agent evaluation, and error propagation
Design minimal POC scope (dataset, perturbations, metrics)
Define success criteria and experimental protocol
notes: Literature review complete with 25 papers surveyed. POC requirements document complete and revised (2026-04-02): (1) Dual benchmarks: ToolBench + GAIA (25 each, 50 total for POC), (2) 3 perturbation types × 3 positions = 9 conditions, (3) Claude-3.5-Sonnet (Bedrock) + GPT-OSS 120B judges, (4) Pre-requisite verification system for datasets/APIs, (5) CCG metric defined, (6) Success criteria: Early CCG < -0.3, Late CCG > +0.2, p < 0.05, (7) Timeline: 2-3 weeks (12-17 hours annotation), (8) Budget: $30-80 (POC), scales to $80-200, (9) Human researcher as annotator, (10) POC-first strategy: validate system with 50, scale to 150+ if promising. Ready for human approval to proceed to implementation.
